disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No constant severe pain, no high fever, no anorexia or persistent vomiting
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain constant and severe, or does it come and go?
Any high fever, loss of appetite, or repeated vomiting?
Does the pain make you not want to eat at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It comes and goes rather than constant, no high fever, my appetite is okay and I haven't vomited.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain markedly worse with movement/coughing or a rigid abdomen (peritonism)
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt much more when you move, cough or press and let go?
Is your tummy hard/rigid, or soft?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a bit worse moving but my tummy feels soft, not rigid, and coughing isn't agony.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Central-to-RLQ migrating colicky pain with loose stools and a history of episodic ileocaecal symptoms
trigger_question_examples: Where did the pain start and how has it moved?
Have you had similar right-sided gut symptoms before, and any change in your bowels?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started centrally then moved to the lower right, I've had milder versions before, and I've had a couple of loose motions.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is it my appendix? Do I need surgery tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen the appendicitis/peritonism red flags; because RLQ pain cannot be examined remotely, arrange urgent same-day in-person assessment rather than confidently reassuring, while explaining a an inflammatory bowel condition flare is plausible. Avoid both dismissing it and asserting it is definitely appendicitis.
